summary: Automated weekly runtest report generator for GPS unit models (N13.5, N13, N12.5). Each model has ~30 units. Report tracks total reporting packets, stored packets, and packets with valid GPS fix per unit, updated weekly. Data source will be Google Sheets (CSV/Excel export from Waggle portal). API integration with Waggle portal planned for future consideration.
architectural_notes: Data source: Google Sheets (provided link references gid=1749308363)
Three unit models: N13.5, N13, N12.5 — each with approximately 30 units
Data pipeline: User exports CSV/Excel from Waggle portal, uploads to Google Sheets, app reads from Google Sheets
Future: Waggle portal REST API integration planned (NimblePet) — defer until API docs are available
File import must support all common formats: CSV, TSV, Excel (XLSX/XLS), and any other tabular formats the Waggle portal may export
known_issues: File upload causes an error after uploading a file (reported in Version 4)

existing_features.title: Build a data entry form that allows the user to select a model (N13.5, N13, N12.5), select or enter a unit ID, specify the week, and input the three packet counts (total reporting packets, stored packets, valid GPS fix packets). The form must support bulk entry for up to ~30 units per model in a single session.

Implement a weekly summary report view that aggregates all unit entries for a selected week and model. The report must display per-unit rows and summary totals for: total reporting packets, stored packets, and valid GPS fix packets. It must also show percentage of valid GPS fix packets out of total packets.

Add a dashboard overview page that shows a high-level summary across all three models for the most recent week: total units reporting, aggregate packet counts, and valid GPS fix rate per model. Include a week navigator to browse historical weekly reports.

Apply a clean, professional visual theme suitable for a technical field-reporting tool. Use a dark navy and amber/orange accent color palette with clear data-table typography. The layout should prioritize data density and readability for tabular data. Apply this theme consistently across all pages.

Add a Google Sheets CSV import feature to the Data Entry page that allows the user to paste a CSV export (from the Waggle portal via Google Sheets) and automatically parse unit IDs and their three packet counts (total reporting packets, stored packets, valid GPS fix packets) for a selected model and week, then bulk-upsert all parsed rows into the backend.

Document the expected CSV column format (column names or order) for the Google Sheets import directly in the UI as a helper text or collapsible instruction block on the Data Entry page, so users know how to prepare their export from the Waggle portal.

Add a bulk CSV file upload mode to the Data Entry page's import section, allowing the user to select and upload multiple CSV files at once (one file per unit). Each uploaded file should be parsed using the existing CSV parsing logic (csvParser.ts) to extract unit ID and the three packet counts. All parsed rows across all uploaded files should be collected and displayed in a combined preview table (matching the existing parse/preview UI pattern in CSVImportSection.tsx) before the user confirms the bulk upsert.

Extend the CSV import section (CSVImportSection.tsx) and its underlying parser (csvParser.ts) to support all common file formats for upload and paste import: CSV (.csv), TSV (.tsv), and Excel/XLSX (.xlsx, .xls). The file picker's accepted file types must include all these extensions. Each uploaded file must be parsed regardless of format to extract unit ID and the three packet counts, then aggregated into the combined preview table before bulk upsert. The paste/text mode should continue to auto-detect CSV vs TSV delimiters as it does today.

Investigate and fix the error that occurs after uploading a file in the CSV import section (CSVImportSection.tsx). The fix must ensure that CSV, TSV, XLS, and XLSX files can all be uploaded and parsed without throwing an error. The root cause should be identified — likely in the SheetJS CDN lazy-loading logic in csvParser.ts, the file reading pipeline, or the error handling path in CSVImportSection.tsx — and resolved so that uploaded files are parsed correctly and their rows appear in the preview table without any error message or broken state.

Rewrite the SheetJS loading mechanism in csvParser.ts to use a bundled or reliably imported SheetJS package (e.g., via npm import) instead of lazy-loading from a CDN, eliminating the CDN fetch failure that causes the upload error.

Fix the file reading pipeline in CSVImportSection.tsx so that after a user selects one or more CSV, TSV, XLS, or XLSX files, each file is read and passed to the parser without throwing or swallowing an unhandled error. Ensure the error boundary and error-display logic in CSVImportSection.tsx correctly distinguishes between a parse warning (malformed row) and a fatal upload error, so users only see a fatal error message when the file truly cannot be processed.

Add defensive error handling around the async file-parsing calls in CSVImportSection.tsx so that if any individual file fails to parse, the error is caught, a per-file error message is shown next to that file's entry in the UI, and the remaining files in the batch continue to be processed and added to the preview table.

new_feature_requests.summary: Support 3 unit models: N13.5, N13, N12.5 with ~30 units each
Weekly report tracking: total reporting packets, stored packets, and valid GPS fix packets per unit
Automate report generation based on Google Sheets data source

Create a backend data model and storage for runtest report entries. Each entry must store: unit model (N13.5, N13, or N12.5), unit ID/serial number, week identifier (ISO week + year), total reporting packets, stored packets, and valid GPS fix packets. Support creating, updating, and retrieving entries.
Import packet data from Google Sheets (exported from Waggle portal) to populate unit report entries automatically
Support bulk CSV file upload: allow user to upload multiple CSV files at once (one per unit), processing all files in a single import session
Support all common file formats for import (CSV, Excel/XLSX, TSV, etc.) — not limited to CSV only
